opp.ais_why:
• On the 92-minute 9/2/2025 workshop, the only AI-tagged commitment is Aakriti Srivastava sending Michael Tutton's team a screenshot of the AI capability for identifying risky clauses in contracts. That is a buyer-pulled AI evaluation, but it sits as one item inside a 12-item action list dominated by supplier onboarding, contract approval flow, SAP adapter integration, FedRAMP, and sub-tier mapping.
• Michael Tutton's own action items are organisational, not technical: lead internal discussions on ARUP's current ERP system capabilities, identify CLM gaps, coordinate with other ARUP stakeholders, and evaluate the need for further demos. He is sizing the CLM scope across ARUP rather than pulling on AI. No second AI-specific buyer ask surfaced across the action set or filtered transcript keyword review.
• AI is flagged Important at the account level and Merlin and Merlin Intake are in product scope. The risky-clause ask plus active engagement keeps this above AI Resistant, but no POC, no AI-tied budget, no named AI champion, and a buyer agenda still focused on CLM gap analysis means it stays at the lower end of AI Curious.

items.quote: legal review runs through external counsel; no MSA/SOW/InfoSec timeline discussed
items.source: Zycus X ARUP CLM Demo 13 Oct 2025 + absence across all sources

items.quote: no cost-of-inaction, cycle-time, or savings figure ever stated
budget owner ('mister Bevan') never on a Zycus call and not in Salesforce contacts
selection/award schedule after 10 Jul not yet shared

sources: Zycus X ARUP CLM Demo 13 Oct 2025
metrics.narrative: Pain is well articulated but never quantified in dollars or hours across the whole call history, a real exposure in a competitive RFP where price and value will be scored. On 16 Oct 2025 Michael Tutton described a fully manual process ('We're just reading it, memorizing, trying to remember what's not there... very manual') and the need to go '7 different places' to manage suppliers and contracts. No cost-of-inaction, cycle-time, or ROI figure has ever been put on the table. Putting a savings model inside the proposal is a ranked move.

champion.narrative: Michael Tutton is a strong, conviction-driven champion who found Zycus himself, openly advocates internally ('Everybody always expects me to say something Zycus' - 09 Jan 2026), and is credited in the Next Step with convincing management to launch the RFP. Ondraya Watkins is a confirmed co-champion focused on supplier risk and obligations. His limits: he admits no prior Zycus experience and forces his own objectivity, and he cannot independently move the skeptical director-level budget owner. Strong at the working level but without power-line access, so making him the economic-buyer broker is the highest-leverage use of his strength.

sources: Zycus X ARUP CLM Demo 13 Oct 2025
competition.narrative: The live threat is incumbent Conga (Salesforce CLM), the tool the RFP exists to test, positioned as 'enhance vs replace' and backed by the director's gap-analysis mandate; do-nothing / enhance-Conga-in-place is the real fallback. Zycus wins on external-counsel Word redlining without portal login, clause library, AI contract discovery and risk scoring, post-contract dashboards, and D365/Merlin AI, the exact gap set Michael cannot close on Conga. Coupa is dormant (a peer-positioning reference only). The wedge is differentiation plus a bundled TCO that exposes the hidden cost of enhancing Conga with separate compare and sign tools.

sources: Zycus X ARUP CLM Demo 13 Oct 2025
identify_pain.narrative: Specific and owner-attributed: a fully manual CLM run through Salesforce/Conga and Word where the team memorizes clause positions and hunts across 7 systems (Michael Tutton, 16 Oct 2025), and Conga treated as a weak repository ('it's not a very good one, but it is a repository'). Ondraya Watkins owns supplier scorecarding and post-contract risk; Arthur Moes owns supplier and minority/woman-owned business risk. Pain is confirmed and multi-owner; its dollar value is not.

sources: Zycus X ARUP CLM Demo 13 Oct 2025
paper_process.narrative: The single biggest close-date blind spot. No MSA, SOW, Order Form, or template has been exchanged and no legal, redline, or InfoSec timeline has been discussed. ARUP signs today through Salesforce/Conga's DocuSign (Michael, 13 Oct 2025), and legal review runs through EXTERNAL counsel that ARUP emails Word documents to, so Zycus paper will route to outside counsel, a step nothing has pre-aligned. With the proposal due 10 Jul and close 31 Jul only ~21 days remain. Pre-staging the MSA/SOW plus InfoSec and the external-counsel redline path now is what protects the date.

economic_buyer.narrative: Budget authority sits with a director referred to on calls only as 'mister Bevan' who mandated the Conga gap analysis and, per Michael, questioned buying the AI module standalone ('can I get the AI module without buying anything else' - 16 Oct 2025). He has never appeared on a Zycus call and is not in the Salesforce contact roles, so his exact name and title are unconfirmed from the relevant stakeholder this champion-authored tender the path to him runs THROUGH Michael Tutton, not around him: arming the champion with a leadership-facing value and ROI narrative is the move, not cold outreach to an unverified contact.

decision_process.narrative: Well mapped from the call sequence: champion-led internal selling, repeated CLM demos, a Bevan-mandated Conga-vs-Zycus gap analysis run with the Salesforce/Conga team over several rounds (Oct 2025 to Feb 2026), an internal executive summary Michael built, and now a formal competitive RFP that is live with multiple suppliers (Mzia Sturua, 24 Jun 2026: 'we are sharing the responses to submitted questions with all suppliers'). The RFP is both a competitive bid and a political compromise letting the director validate the champion objectively. The post-proposal selection and award timeline is the critical unknown.

sources: Zycus X ARUP CLM Demo 13 Oct 2025
decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are clear and now codified in the RFP against Conga's gap set: side-by-side compare and redlining, clause and template library, restricted clause-level editing, external-counsel Word collaboration without portal login, AI contract discovery and Insta-review risk scoring, post-contract obligation and utilization dashboards, and D365/Merlin integration. On 13 Oct 2025 Robert Stipanovich probed licensing for external counsel and suppliers and Ondraya Watkins probed supplier risk scoring, showing the evaluation is criteria-led. The stated bar is explicitly what Conga cannot do.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. It's genuinely competitive and could go either way; the reads below say where it stands.

blocks.how: A deal at 'Shortlisted' anchors near 55. Win signals and momentum lift the read to 64.
blocks.read: We're in it, with a slight edge.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: Champion is driving it — Michael Tutton is a strong, conviction-driven champion credited with getting the RFP launched, but he lacks power-line access to the skeptical budget owner and admits no prior Zycus experience. Making him the economic-buyer broker with a quantified value narrative is the highest-leverage use of his strength.
bullets.text: Champion is driving it — Michael Tutton is a strong, conviction-driven champion credited with getting the RFP launched, but he lacks power-line access to the skeptical budget…
Live next steps with named dates

blocks.read: There's a live competitive threat plus execution risk — both below.
blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.footer: Competitive picture: Conga (Salesforce CLM) is the single strongest current threat: the director-sponsored incumbent the RFP exists to validate, positioned as lower-risk 'enhance vs replace.' Zycus wins on external-counsel Word redlining without portal login…

bullets.text: No pricing, TCO, or ROI anchored; the director's module-bundling cost concern is unanswered going into the proposal
Budget owner ('Bevan') skeptical, never on a Zycus call, and sponsors the incumbent gap analysis
31 Jul close behind a 10 Jul proposal deadline with selection, pricing, legal, and InfoSec unstarted; five close-date pushes since Mar 2026
External-counsel redline path and Zycus MSA/SOW/InfoSec never pre-aligned

items: Submit the CLM RFP proposal by 10 Jul leading with the gaps Conga cannot close. (by 9 Jul)
Anchor a bundled iContract, iSupplier and Merlin TCO in the proposal to answer the module-cost concern. (by 9 Jul)
Equip Michael Tutton with a one-page quantified ROI narrative to carry to the budget owner. (by 15 Jul)
blocks.title: What moves it forward

contributions.evidence: Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found.

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 21 is driven by Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 64 is carried by preference strength +1.00 (weight 20); champion strength +1.00 (weight 15). Offsetting it: competitive strength -1.00 (weight 15).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 64 (moving forward): Next Step carries 13 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (stage integrity, exec and champion, commercial, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 47 is anchored on the stage win (64), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 64, and dragged by risk 21. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Verdict flags close-date risk this sweep.
Next Step carries 13 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 4.0 / Moderate reflects genuine but shallow AI pull: a single risky-clause AI ask inside a 12-item CLM action list, no POC, no AI-tied budget, no named AI champion. Calls confirm the field read, not an under-rating.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain only (fully manual CLM, hunting across 7 systems, CFO-level version-control frustration); no ROI, savings, or cycle-time number ever stated.

items.change: Formal Source-to-Settle CLM RFP released with dated milestones (Intent 06/09, Questions 06/16, Proposal 07/10)
ARUP issued consolidated RFP question responses to all participating suppliers
Zycus confirmed intent to participate and submitted its RFP questions
CloseDate pushed 06/30 to 07/31 (fifth push since Mar 2026)

deal_movement.summary: Reads from Next_Step_History, field history, and the last real buyer touches: the deal moved from champion-led internal selling to a live, formal competitive RFP, while the close date was pushed five times since March 2026 and the buyer engagement is now email/RFP-based rather than call-based.

critical_signals.text: The evaluation is now a formal multi-supplier CLM RFP built to test incumbent Conga, with Zycus winning only on the redlining, clause-library, AI risk-scoring, and dashboard gaps Conga cannot close.
ARUP issued consolidated RFP answers to all suppliers on 24 Jun, confirming Zycus is a live bidder against a 10 Jul proposal deadline.
No pricing, TCO, or ROI has ever been anchored, leaving the module-bundling cost concern the director raised unanswered going into the proposal.
Mzia Sturua now runs the RFP as a neutral procurement gatekeeper and is the primary channel we are engaged with, while the budget owner has still never joined a Zycus call.

items: Champion drove management to release a formal RFP (6/29)
Buyer issued consolidated RFP answers to all suppliers (6/24)
Latest transcribed buyer call is 04 Feb 2026; recent touches are RFP emails
confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is live but has shifted from calls to email/RFP process; the champion is active and the RFP is progressing, so the deal is not cooling, but the last true buyer call was 04 Feb 2026.

north_star_verdict.math: Today 2026-07-03, close 2026-07-31 (28 days out). RFP proposal due 07/10/2026 (7 days out) then selection, finalist review, pricing, legal and InfoSec must all complete in ~21 days. Buyer's own mid-Feb decision target slipped ~4 months and CloseDate has been pushed five times since Mar 2026 (last 06/30 to 07/31 on 2026-06-03). Stage Shortlisted, not yet Vendor Selected or Negotiation. Last buyer touch 2026-06-24 (incoming RFP email). The date is optimistic, the deal is not.
evidence: SF Next_Step (6/29): RFP for CLM released, Proposal Submission 07/10/2026
SF field history: CloseDate 06/30 -> 07/31 on 2026-06-03, one of five pushes since Mar 2026
Michael Tutton (04 Feb 2026 call): 'it's still gonna be a hard sell'
north_star_verdict.headline: The champion-authored CLM RFP is genuinely live and engaged, but a 31 Jul close cannot survive a 10 Jul proposal deadline with no pricing anchored, no MSA or InfoSec pre-work, and external counsel still to redline.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Best Case (not Commit): RFP just issued, no pricing/MSA/InfoSec, realistic award likely beyond 31 Jul

flags: Win the RFP on Conga's gap set, not feature parity: lead the proposal with external-counsel Word redlining, clause library, AI risk scoring, and D365/Merlin.
Convert Michael Tutton into the economic-buyer broker: equip him with a quantified ROI and TCO narrative for the skeptical director who scores the RFP.
Protect the 31 Jul date now: pre-stage MSA/SOW, InfoSec, and the external-counsel redline path before award, not after.
best_practice_check.summary: Win-strategy levers for a live, champion-authored RFP where the budget owner is skeptical and the date is compressed.

customer_preference.evidence: Michael Tutton found Zycus himself and openly advocates internally: 'Everybody always expects me to say something Zycus... he's an advocate, he loves it' (09 Jan 2026), and drove management to launch the RFP.

competitive_position.summary: Conga (Salesforce CLM) is the single strongest current threat: the director-sponsored incumbent the RFP exists to validate, positioned as lower-risk 'enhance vs replace.' Zycus wins on external-counsel Word redlining without portal login, clause library, AI risk scoring, post-contract dashboards, and D365/Merlin. Do-nothing/enhance-Conga is the active fallback to beat on differentiation and bundled price. Coupa is dormant, surfacing only as Gartner peer-positioning. The field is now a formal multi-supplier RFP, so proposal quality and price decide it.

competitors.name: Conga (Salesforce CLM)
competitors.quote: their vision is still the Conga is a repository. In my opinion, it's not a very good one, but it is a repository
competitors.how_we_win: Lead the RFP response with the gap set Conga cannot close (external-counsel Word redlining without portal login, clause library, AI contract discovery and risk scoring, post-contract dashboards, D365/Merlin) plus a bundled TCO exposing the hidden cost of enhancing Conga with separate compare/sign tools.

competitors.name: Do-nothing / enhance Conga in-place
competitors.quote: can I get the AI module without buying anything else
competitors.how_we_win: Anchor replacement value against the hidden cost of enhancing Conga plus point tools; make the totality-of-solution TCO the centerpiece.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.source: Catch up with Zycus 09 Jan 2026 (peer-positioning context)
competitors.how_we_win: Not an active bidder; reinforce the Gartner Magic Quadrant peer positioning Michael already values.

items.quote: ARUP Laboratories, Inc. is seeking the best strategic partner and solution to support its enterprise contract... Proposal Submission Deadline: 07/10/2026
items.requirement: Submit the Zycus proposal to the Source-to-Settle CLM RFP by 10 Jul 2026

items.deliverable: Share the post-proposal selection and award timeline after the 10 Jul submission
items.grounding_quote: He will share Project timeline and next steps once final approval has been granted

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI (Merlin/Certinal) is in scope and demoed (contract discovery, Insta-review risk scoring, negotiation genie), but the buyer agenda stayed CLM gap analysis and only one AI-specific ask surfaced. The agentic story is present but under-positioned versus its potential.

We send it through the Salesforce for Conga, which has a Docusign
items.source: Zycus X ARUP CLM Demo 13 Oct 2025
items.criterion: Fast, clear legal/paper path to signature

customer_expectations_fit.summary: ARUP expects a CLM that beats Conga on authoring, external-counsel collaboration, AI risk scoring, and post-contract management, and that bundles supplier and intake modules without runaway per-module cost. Zycus is aligned on capability but exposed on price transparency and on the paper/legal path.

hard.ais_why:
• On the 92-minute 9/2/2025 workshop, the only AI-tagged commitment is Aakriti Srivastava sending Michael Tutton's team a screenshot of the AI capability for identifying risky clauses in contracts. That is a buyer-pulled AI evaluation, but it sits as one item inside a 12-item action list dominated by supplier onboarding, contract approval flow, SAP adapter integration, FedRAMP, and sub-tier mapping.
• Michael Tutton's own action items are organisational, not technical: lead internal discussions on ARUP's current ERP system capabilities, identify CLM gaps, coordinate with other ARUP stakeholders, and evaluate the need for further demos. He is sizing the CLM scope across ARUP rather than pulling on AI. No second AI-specific buyer ask surfaced across the action set or filtered transcript keyword review.
• AI is flagged Important at the account level and Merlin and Merlin Intake are in product scope. The risky-clause ask plus active engagement keeps this above AI Resistant, but no POC, no AI-tied budget, no named AI champion, and a buyer agenda still focused on CLM gap analysis means it stays at the lower end of AI Curious.
